name: Galacho de Juslibol y Muralla de Grisén — circular Camino Natural del Ebro (GR 99) y Canal Imperial de Aragón
region_intro: Mountain biking around Zaragoza offers a diverse range of landscapes, from arid steppes and riverine ecosystems to mountain foothills. The region is characterized by varied terrain, including paths along the Ebro River, trails through semi-arid plains like Los Monegros, and routes leading to higher elevations such as the Moncayo Natural Park. This geographical variety provides a wide array of mountain bike trails suitable for different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Zaragoza The most popular mountain bike trail is Camino de la Estepa (CR41) — old road from Zaragoza to Torrecilla de Valmadrid, a 24.8 miles (39.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 36 minutes to complete. This moderate route traverses open steppe landscapes. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is South Green Ring of Zaragoza, a moderate 20.9 miles (33.6 km) path. This circular route follows the Ebro River and the Imperial Canal of Aragon, offering scenic riverine views. Local mountain bikers also love the Galacho de Juslibol y Muralla de Grisén — circular Camino Natural del Ebro (GR 99) y Canal Imperial de Aragón, a 46.4 miles (74.7 km) trail leading through riverine ecosystems and along the Ebro, often completed in about 5 hours 23 minutes. Mountain biking around Zaragoza is defined by arid steppes, riverine paths, and mountain foothills. The extensive network offers options for various ability levels, from challenging enduro trails to easier cross-country routes. The routes in Zaragoza are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 1000 reviews. More than 11000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Zaragoza's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in Zaragoza?
A: Zaragoza offers a wide selection of mountain bike trails, with over 560 routes available on komoot. These trails cater to various skill levels, including easy, moderate, and difficult options.

Q: Are there easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Zaragoza?
A: Yes, Zaragoza has over 100 easy mountain bike trails perfect for beginners. Many of these routes follow the Ebro River or the Imperial Canal of Aragon, offering gentle terrain and scenic views. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984655214/north-zaragoza-greenbelt-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;North Zaragoza Greenbelt Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 30 km ride.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while mountain biking around Zaragoza?
A: The region around Zaragoza boasts incredibly diverse landscapes. You can ride through arid steppes, lush riverine ecosystems along the Ebro River, and even venture into mountain foothills like those leading to Moncayo Natural Park. Areas like Los Monegros Desert offer a unique semi-arid experience, while routes along the Ebro provide serene settings.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike routes near Zaragoza?
A: Yes, for those seeking longer rides, Zaragoza offers several extensive routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988074991/galacho-de-juslibol-y-muralla-de-grisen-circular-camino-natural-del-ebro-gr-99-y-canal-imperial-de-aragon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Galacho de Juslibol y Muralla de Grisén — circular Camino Natural del Ebro (GR 99) y Canal Imperial de Aragón&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 74.7 km trail that takes you through riverine ecosystems. Another option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e985042256/sendas-urcamusa-la-muela-desde-los-alrededores-de-zaragoza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendas Urcamusa – La Muela desde los alrededores de Zaragoza&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 52.3 km route.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in Zaragoza?
A: Absolutely. Many mountain bike trails in Zaragoza are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984519234/south-green-ring-of-zaragoza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;South Green Ring of Zaragoza&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 33.6 km path that follows the Ebro River and the Imperial Canal of Aragon.

Q: What are some scenic mountain bike trails with good viewpoints in Zaragoza?
A: For scenic views, consider routes that venture towards higher ground or along the rivers. The Moncayo Natural Park, though further afield, offers panoramic views from its trails. Closer to the city, routes along the Ebro River provide picturesque riverine vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984622393/barranco-de-las-almunias-collado-sillon-mirador-de-las-planas-circular-desde-cadrete" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco de las Almunias - Collado Sillón - Mirador de las Planas&lt;/a&gt; route offers elevated perspectives.

Q: Are there advanced or challenging mountain bike trails in Zaragoza?
A: Yes, Zaragoza caters to experienced mountain bikers with nearly 100 difficult trails. The Moncayo Natural Park, for instance, features challenging enduro trails and demanding XC/rally routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983654182/monte-oscuro-y-san-caprasio-circular-por-los-montes-pedriguera-y-la-sierra-de-alcubierre" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Oscuro y San Caprasio&lt;/a&gt; route is a moderate-to-difficult option with significant elevation gain.

Q: What is the best season for mountain biking in Zaragoza?
A: The best seasons for mountain biking in Zaragoza are typically spring and autumn. During these times, the weather is mild and pleasant, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter conditions of winter. This allows for comfortable riding across the diverse landscapes.

Q: What attractions or landmarks can I explore near the mountain bike trails?
A: Zaragoza's surroundings offer various attractions. You can find beautiful lakes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5238060" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balsa de Larralde&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1371792" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Lake&lt;/a&gt;. Historical sites include the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4533468" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aljafería Palace&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6074130" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Walls and Torreón de la Zuda&lt;/a&gt;. The Monasterio de Piedra Natural Park, though a bit further, is renowned for its stunning waterfalls.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Zaragoza?
A: The komoot community highly rates mountain biking in Zaragoza, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 1000 reviews. Riders often praise the region's diverse terrain, from the unique arid steppes to the tranquil river paths, and the extensive network of trails that cater to all abilities.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails with parking available?
A: Many trailheads and popular starting points for mountain bike routes around Zaragoza offer parking facilities. For example, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984605939/salada-de-mediana-and-canal-imperial-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salada de Mediana and Canal Imperial Loop&lt;/a&gt; often have accessible parking nearby, especially in smaller towns or natural park entrances.

Q: Can I find less crowded mountain bike trails in Zaragoza?
A: While popular routes can see more traffic, Zaragoza's extensive network of over 560 trails means you can often find quieter options. Exploring routes further from the immediate city center, such as those in Montes de Castejón like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e987182400/montes-de-castejon-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montes de Castejón Loop&lt;/a&gt;, or venturing into areas like Los Monegros, can offer a more solitary riding experience.
tips: Extraordinary place to play sports and enjoy the native flora and fauna. There are picnic sites and different paths for walking or cycling. On weekends it can be a bit crowded, so BEWARE!
From the Soto de Malfora we will have magnificent views of the Ebro River that will undoubtedly tempt us to take out the camera to photograph it.
Historic arch bridge built in the 18th century to cross the Imperial Canal of Aragon. Nice picture to photograph!

La Balsa de Larralde or Torre Medina is a wetland located in the municipality of Zaragoza, in the neighborhood of Torre Medina, belonging to the rural neighborhood of Garrapinillos. This pond, together with the Ojo del Cura and El Fraile, are part of an old set of lagoons that surround the neighborhoods of Casetas and Garrapinillos and that were formed by the subsidence of the land since the subsoil plasters were diluted by groundwater. and they end up collapsing forming sinkholes.

La Balsa de Larralde, recovered by the Ebro Hydrographic Confederation (CHE) and by the Aragon Naturalist Association, is a 4-hectare wetland with a great and rich variety of flora and fauna. It has a maximum depth of 5 meters.

La Balsa is surrounded by poplars and reedbeds. The latter forms a belt that protects the raft as aquatic vegetation. Fundamentally it is formed by two species of plants: the reed itself and the cattail. This environment is used by a diversity of animals to take refuge, search for food or build their nests.

waypoints.name: Clavería Bridge over the Imperial Canal of Aragon
